name: Ace of Swords
uprightMeaning: A blade of clarity cutting through confusion. The Ace of Swords is a moment of intellectual breakthrough — clarity of thought, decisive insight, the truth that sets free.
reversedMeaning: Confusion, misinformation, tactical confusion. The blade is dull or turned inward — clarity corrupted into obsession.

name: Two of Swords
uprightMeaning: The stalemate. Two of Swords depicts a figure in careful equilibrium — a decision deliberately postponed, emotions deliberately ignored.
reversedMeaning: Inaction, stalemate, confusion. The crossroad is missed — or the decision is made through avoidance rather than choice.

name: Three of Swords
uprightMeaning: The wound that teaches. Three of Swords is heartbreak in its rawest form — grief that must be felt fully before it can be released.
reversedMeaning: Recovery, forgiveness, releasing grief. The blade is being withdrawn — healing begins when the wound is finally cleaned.

name: Four of Swords
uprightMeaning: The necessary retreat. Four of Swords is forced or chosen withdrawal — a period of rest, recovery, and quiet preparation for what comes next.
reversedMeaning: Exhaustion, burn-out, deep contemplation. The rest has gone on too long — or the stillness was never deep enough to heal.
symbolism: knight lying on tomb with four swords above

name: Five of Swords
uprightMeaning: Pyrrhic victory. Five of Swords shows a winner who has already lost — pride that cost more than it gained, a battle won but the war surrendered.
reversedMeaning: Reconciliation, making amends, past resentment. The tide turns — or the wisdom to stop fighting before everything is lost.
symbolism: figure retrieving swords from two fallen opponents

name: Six of Swords
uprightMeaning: The crossing. Six of Swords depicts passage through troubled waters — a transition undertaken, a difficult chapter being left behind, healing underway.
reversedMeaning: Stuck in the past, resisting change, unfinished business. The passage is blocked — or the departure is made before readiness.
symbolism: figure in boat with six swords moving to calmer water

name: Seven of Swords
uprightMeaning: The shadow play. Seven of Swords shows cunning in motion — strategy that crosses ethical lines, a secret kept through cleverness rather than honesty.
reversedMeaning: Confession, coming clean, imprisonment by one's own actions. The secret unravels — or the truth is chosen over escape.
symbolism: figure slipping away with five swords in the night

name: Eight of Swords
uprightMeaning: The prison of the mind. Eight of Swords depicts binding self-imposed — restriction imagined more than real, a prison whose walls dissolve when examined.
reversedMeaning: Opening up to new perspectives, freedom, release. The blindfold falls — the bindings loosen when courage returns.
symbolism: figure bound and blindfolded amid eight swords stuck in the ground

name: Nine of Swords
uprightMeaning: The weight of a racing mind. Nine of Swords shows the dark hour before dawn — anxiety amplified into physical pain, the night when fears become monsters.
reversedMeaning: Hope, reaching out, recovery. The light returns — or accepting that the worst has not happened and may never happen.
symbolism: figure sitting on bed with nine swords overhead

name: Ten of Swords
uprightMeaning: The worst has happened. Ten of Swords marks rock bottom — the final blow, the absolute end, the moment from which the only direction is up.
reversedMeaning: Recovery, regeneration, resistance. The body recovers before the spirit does — but it does recover. Sunrise after the longest night.
symbolism: figure face-down with ten swords in their back

name: Page of Swords
uprightMeaning: The eager student of truth. The Page of Swords arrives with burning curiosity — a mind that must know, questions that demand answers, an idea that will not rest.
reversedMeaning: Scattered thoughts, defensiveness, all talk no action. The mind races but the feet stand still — or anger twisted into cruelty.
symbolism: figure standing alert with sword and scroll

name: Knight of Swords
uprightMeaning: Charge of the intellect. The Knight of Swords moves with fierce ambition — direct, driven, sometimes reckless, cutting through obstacles with pure will.
reversedMeaning: Recklessness, haste, ruthlessness. The charge goes wrong — or the ambition is aimed at the wrong target entirely.
symbolism: armored knight on charging horse with sword raised

name: Queen of Swords
uprightMeaning: The razor mind. The Queen of Swords rules with sharp perception — direct, honest, emotionally detached, a mind that cuts through pretense to find truth.
reversedMeaning: Cold-hearted, cruel, defensiveness. The blade is turned malicious — intelligence weaponized into harm, or a broken heart that refuses to feel.
symbolism: throned queen with sword and severed head

name: King of Swords
uprightMeaning: Throne of the mind. The King of Swords commands through pure intellect — authority earned by wisdom, power wielded with precision, truth spoken without fear.
reversedMeaning: Abuse of power, tyranny, manipulation. The crown becomes a weapon — intellect in service of ego, authority corrupted into cruelty.
symbolism: enthroned king with sword raised and book
dramatic sky and throne of stone
